summary: Production Management App (Production Manager) is a web/PWA application backed by an Internet Computer (Motoko) canister for tracking manufacturing production. It targets Android usage via PWA/TWA APK packaging and includes master-data management (products, machines, operators), a production data-entry form with runtime/target/operator-hour calculations, and an admin reports interface (filtering, summaries, export/share, deletion). The conversation introduced plans to run the app without Internet Identity login (direct access), add additional production fields (pieces-per-cycle, parts-produced, downtime, punch in/out & duty time), extend reporting (date-range, operator/product summaries), improve large dropdown usability and master-data sorting, and improve mobile/TWA export+share behavior.

existing_features.title: Tab-based application shell with theme toggle
existing_features.description: Provides the main UI structure with a header, footer, and tab navigation between Data Entry and Admin sections. Includes dark/light theme support via next-themes and a theme toggle button.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication provider and session identity restoration
existing_features.description: Implements an InternetIdentityProvider that initializes AuthClient, restores existing sessions, supports login/logout, and exposes identity + status flags to the app.

existing_features.title: Backend actor creation with identity and secure secret parameter handling
existing_features.description: Creates an authenticated canister actor when an identity exists, calls backend access-control initialization with a secret from the URL hash/session, and invalidates/refetches React Query caches when the actor changes. Includes utilities to extract secrets from URL hash and clear them to reduce leakage.

existing_features.title: Role-based access-control bootstrap and role management (backend)
existing_features.description: Backend AccessControl allows an initial admin to be assigned using an environment admin token and a user-provided secret; supports role lookup, admin check, and admin-only role assignment via MixinAuthorization.

existing_features.title: User profile storage and retrieval (backend)
existing_features.description: Stores per-principal user profiles (name/role text). Users can save their own profile and query their own profile; admins can query other users’ profiles.

existing_features.title: Products master-data management with sorting and uniqueness validation
existing_features.description: Backend supports create/update/delete/list for products with unique-name enforcement and multiple server-side sorted list endpoints. Frontend provides full CRUD UI with client-side sorting controls, duplicate name checks, optimistic updates, and fields including loadingTime, unloadingTime, piecesPerCycle, and cycleTime.

existing_features.title: Machines master-data management with sorting and uniqueness validation
existing_features.description: Backend supports create/update/delete/list for machines with unique-name enforcement and optional server-side sorting by name. Frontend provides CRUD UI with client-side sorting, optimistic updates, and duplicate name checks.

existing_features.title: Operators master-data management with sorting and uniqueness validation
existing_features.description: Backend supports create/update/delete/list for operators with unique-name enforcement and optional server-side sorting by name. Frontend provides CRUD UI with client-side sorting, optimistic updates, and duplicate name checks.

existing_features.title: Production data entry form with real-time calculations
existing_features.description: Provides a production entry form to select machine/operator/product and input cycle time, quantity, downtime info, and punch in/out times. Calculates number of parts produced, 10-hour and 12-hour targets, duty time, total machine run time, and total operator hours in real time before submitting to the backend.

existing_features.title: Production entry persistence and computed metrics (backend)
existing_features.description: Backend stores production entries with computed metrics including numberOfPartsProduced, tenHourTarget, twelveHourTarget, totalRunTime, dutyTime, and totalOperatorHours, derived from product times, cycle time, downtime, and punch in/out times.

existing_features.title: Production entry querying and deletion
existing_features.description: Backend supports querying all entries and filtering by date range, operator, or product, plus deleting entries by ID. Frontend provides React Query hooks for these queries and mutations with cache invalidation and toast notifications.

existing_features.title: Admin reports viewer with filters, summaries, export and delete actions
existing_features.description: Admin UI provides multiple report types (all/daily/monthly/date-range/machine/operator/product/operator+date-range/operator summary/product summary), computes summary statistics, displays tabular results, allows deleting entries, and exports reports as CSV or HTML (print-to-PDF workflow) with robust save/share/view handling for mobile/TWA environments.

existing_features.title: Progressive Web App (PWA) support with offline caching
existing_features.description: Includes a PWA manifest, icons, and a service worker that precaches critical assets and provides runtime caching (network-first with cache fallback) for improved reliability/offline behavior.

existing_features.title: Android Trusted Web Activity (TWA) packaging assets and documentation
existing_features.description: Provides assetlinks.json for Android domain verification and a setup guide (TWA_SETUP.md) to package the deployed PWA as an Android APK using Trusted Web Activity.

existing_features.title: Caffeine blob-storage maintenance mixin (backend)
existing_features.description: Includes backend mixins for Caffeine blob storage operations: updating authorized gateway principals, checking blob liveness, listing dead blobs to delete, confirming deletions with GC trigger, creating upload certificates, and refilling a cashier canister with cycles under authorization rules.

existing_features.title: Stable storage and upgrade migration for backend state
existing_features.description: Backend state for core entities (e.g., products, machines, operators, production entries, user profiles, and authorization/access-control state) is persisted across canister upgrades using stable storage and explicit migration/upgrade hooks, with a migration module to evolve stored schema safely.

existing_features.title: Duty Time calculation with <12h lunch-break deduction (frontend + backend)
existing_features.description: Duty Time is derived from Punch In/Punch Out with a business rule: if (PunchOut − PunchIn) < 12h then Duty Time = duration − 30 minutes; otherwise no 30-minute deduction. The adjusted Duty Time is used consistently across UI display and backend-stored ProductionEntry computations.

existing_features.title: Total Operator Hours formula updated and shared across frontend + backend
existing_features.description: Replaces prior operator-hours logic with the user-specified formula based on adjusted Duty Time and shift targets: if Duty Time ≤ 10h then (QuantityProduced / TenHourTarget) * 10; else (QuantityProduced / TwelveHourTarget) * 12. Implemented for real-time Data Entry display and persisted identically in backend ProductionEntry.totalOperatorHours.

existing_features.title: Reports display/export Total Operator Hours from stored entry value (no report-side recomputation)
synonyms: Single source of truth operator hours
existing_features.description: Reports UI and CSV/HTML exports use ProductionEntry.totalOperatorHours as stored on each entry and do not recalculate Total Operator Hours in the reporting codepath.

existing_features.title: No-login (personal mode) app access with removed admin gating for master-data CRUD
existing_features.description: App can be used without Internet Identity login (direct/anonymous access). Master-data CRUD (products, machines, operators) no longer requires admin privileges, enabling full personal-use workflows without authentication prompts or authorization blocks.

existing_features.title: Sign-in/login UI removed; app always opens in no-login/personal mode
existing_features.description: Frontend no longer exposes Sign In / Internet Identity login prompts in the UI. The app opens directly in personal/no-login mode while still supporting core workflows via an anonymous canister actor.

existing_features.title: Post-mutation UI refresh via React Query invalidation for entries and master data
existing_features.description: Create/update/delete mutations for production entries and master data (products/machines/operators) now invalidate/refetch the relevant React Query caches so newly added/edited items appear immediately in UI lists, dropdowns, and reports without requiring a manual reload.

existing_features.title: Ensure the Total Operator Hours value stored on each ProductionEntry in the backend matches the Total Operator Hours value calculated and shown in the Data Entry form, so that the Reports table/exported reports display the exact same operator-hours value for the same entry.

new_feature_requests.summary: Add product master-data field: Pieces per Cycle; in data-entry add Number of Parts Produced auto-calculated as Pieces per Cycle × Quantity Produced (stored and shown in reports/exports)
Add downtime capture on data-entry (reason + duration) and ensure downtime is included in runtime/operator-hour calculations (but NOT multiplied by quantity)
Add attendance fields in data-entry: Punch In, Punch Out, and auto-calculated Duty Time; include in reports/exports

Add/adjust 10-hour and 12-hour target calculations based on form-entered cycle time (including loading+unloading) with configurable reduction factor; show targets as separate fields and include in reports
Implement Total Operator Hours field with configurable business rules (duty-time threshold logic, downtime inclusion, target-achieved overrides) and include per-operator summaries over a date range
Reporting enhancements: add report types for custom date range, report by date, report by product, operator-by-date-range report, and product/operator summaries for a selected date range
Mobile/TWA export improvements: ensure CSV/PDF export works on Android (APK/TWA) and add sharing (Web Share/intent) plus reliable save location/"View file" flow
Master-data UX: add sorting in master-data tables; ensure dropdowns support large lists with scrolling and optionally stable ordering (e.g., serial/ID order)
Add derived cycle-time fields for 10h/12h targets (displayed as minutes:seconds) based on provided formulas and keep formatting consistent across UI and reports

Obtain clarification from the user on the exact three changes they want made (including the affected screen/feature, the desired new behavior, and any formulas/fields involved) before starting implementation; do not make speculative changes based only on prior deployments.

Align backend operator-hours business logic with the frontend operator-hours utility: (1) use the same duty-time threshold rule as the form (≤ 10 hours uses 10-hour target formula; > 10 hours uses 12-hour target formula), (2) compute Total Operator Hours with fractional precision (minutes/seconds) instead of integer hours-only division, and (3) keep Duty Time lunch-break deduction consistent with the form rule (deduct 30 minutes only when raw duration is < 12 hours).

architecturalNotes: Frontend is React + TypeScript, component/page layout with tab-based navigation (Data Entry vs Admin Dashboard).
TanStack React Query is used as the data layer (queries/mutations, cache invalidation, optimistic updates, staleTime tuning).
Backend is a Motoko actor canister exposing query and shared update methods; data is stored in in-memory Maps keyed by Nat IDs with incrementing counters.
Authentication uses DFINITY Internet Identity on the frontend (AuthClient/DelegationIdentity), and the authenticated identity is injected into the canister actor via agentOptions.

Authorization is implemented as a reusable Motoko mixin (AccessControl state + MixinAuthorization) with a 'first admin' bootstrap using an env var token and a user-provided secret passed from the frontend.
Frontend secret handling uses URL hash extraction + sessionStorage persistence and clears sensitive hash params from the URL to reduce token leakage.

Reporting is computed client-side from fetched entries (filtering, aggregation) and exported via generated CSV/HTML blobs; includes device-specific save/share fallbacks (File System Access API → download → Web Share API).
PWA support includes manifest, icons, theme metadata, and a service worker with precache + runtime cache (network-first with cache fallback).
Android TWA distribution is supported via assetlinks.json and a packaging guide.
Backend includes a Caffeine blob-storage maintenance mixin with authorization checks for blob pruning workflows and cashier refill support.

Build template infrastructure was updated (React/Vite workspace, scripts for pruning/resizing images, and custom ESLint rules such as avoiding BigInt in React Query keys and requiring the Internet Identity provider wrapper).
UI layer now incorporates shadcn/ui component primitives (Tailwind-based) as a shared design system; many prebuilt components live under src/components/ui and are consumed by app pages/dialogs.

Backend now includes a migration/upgrade layer (preupgrade/postupgrade + migration module) to persist application state in stable storage across canister upgrades and to support future schema evolution.
Add a 'no-login/personal mode' option: bypass Internet Identity and adjust backend/authorization expectations accordingly (especially for master-data CRUD and deletion actions).
Exports on mobile/TWA should use mobile-compatible save/share flows (Web Share API, Android intent-style sharing, and robust fallbacks when traditional downloads/file pickers fail).
Time-entry UX requires consistent units/formatting (cycle time in minutes+seconds where requested; runtime in hours+minutes) and shared formatting helpers for UI + reports.

Total Operator Hours business rule update: if Duty Time ≤ 10h then TotalOperatorHours = (QuantityProduced / TenHourTarget) * 10; if Duty Time > 10h then TotalOperatorHours = (QuantityProduced / TwelveHourTarget) * 12 (ensure consistent units/rounding).
Duty Time adjustment rule update: if (PunchOut − PunchIn) < 12h then Duty Time = (PunchOut − PunchIn) − 30 minutes; otherwise no 30-minute deduction.
Reporting requirement: reports must use the same Total Operator Hours value/rules as shown in the form data (no divergent report-side recomputation).

Total Operator Hours business rule (confirmed): if Duty Time ≤ 10h then TotalOperatorHours = (QuantityProduced / TenHourTarget) * 10; if Duty Time > 10h then TotalOperatorHours = (QuantityProduced / TwelveHourTarget) * 12 (ensure consistent units/rounding).
Duty Time adjustment rule (confirmed): if (PunchOut − PunchIn) < 12h then Duty Time = (PunchOut − PunchIn) − 30 minutes; otherwise no 30-minute deduction.
Reporting requirement (confirmed): reports must display the exact Total Operator Hours value stored/calculated for the entry (single source of truth; no report-side recomputation).
Frontend and backend share a single, aligned Duty Time + Total Operator Hours business-rules implementation (via shared frontend helpers and mirrored backend logic) to avoid discrepancies.
Reports and exports treat ProductionEntry.totalOperatorHours as the single source of truth and do not recompute operator hours in reporting.
Scope clarification: remove admin restrictions completely (no admin gating) for master-data CRUD (machines/products/operators) so the app works fully without login for personal use.
Performance focus: optimize sluggishness specifically when loading the data-entry form and when adding machines (admin/master-data workflows).

User explicitly approved proceeding with (1) completely removing all admin restrictions for master-data CRUD and (2) performance optimization focused on data-entry form load and adding machines workflows.
Authentication is now optional: the frontend supports operating without an Internet Identity session (personal/anonymous mode) and still creates a functional canister actor for core workflows.
Scope implemented: admin restrictions were removed/relaxed so master-data CRUD (machines/products/operators) is no longer gated by admin privileges, enabling no-login personal use.

UI/reporting: remove/replace any 'Avg Runtime' metric with 'Total Operator Hours' where requested, and ensure report-side 'Operator Hours' is displayed from stored ProductionEntry.totalOperatorHours (no recomputation).

Confirmed scope: Reports (ALL report types) and exports (CSV/PDF/HTML) must display Operator Hours by directly using the stored ProductionEntry.totalOperatorHours value as the single source of truth (no report-side recomputation).
Confirmed scope: Remove 'Avg Runtime' everywhere it appears (report tables, summary cards/sections, and all exports) and replace it with 'Total Operator Hours' in the same positions/roles.
Confirmed scope: Fully remove the Sign In option and all Internet Identity login UI prompts so the app always opens in no-login/personal mode.

10-hour/12-hour target formula decision: compute targets from the Production Entry form’s effective cycle time (form cycle time + product loading + unloading), using constants 34200 (9.5h in seconds) and 39600 (11h in seconds): TenHourTarget = (34200 / effectiveCycleTime) * 0.95 and TwelveHourTarget = (39600 / effectiveCycleTime) * 0.95. Targets are reduced by 5% via multiplier 0.95 and are shown in form + reports.

React Query mutation success handlers now consistently invalidate/refetch the relevant list/report queries so newly created production entries and master-data records appear immediately across the UI.
knownIssues: Most backend CRUD endpoints (products, machines, operators, entries) do not enforce AccessControl roles/permissions; any caller can modify data unless guarded elsewhere.
Environment variable name for storage cashier principal appears misspelled as "CAFFFEINE_STORAGE_CASHIER_PRINCIPAL" (extra 'F'); this is likely to cause deployment/configuration errors.
Two different CSS theme files are shown (frontend/src/index.css and frontend/index.css) with conflicting variable sets; potential duplication/misconfiguration depending on build setup.
On Android (especially APK/TWA), CSV/PDF export reports may not actually save to accessible storage; 'downloaded' messages and 'View File' actions can be non-functional.
Reports and report calculations have been repeatedly reported as not generating or not matching expected formulas; needs verification and correction.
Performance: app can become sluggish/slow to load after multiple feature additions.
App is sluggish/slow and not responsive during normal use; performance needs optimization (load time + UI interactions).
Performance: app is sluggish specifically when loading the data-entry form and when adding machines in admin/master data screens.

BUG: Reports still show 'Op Hours/Operator Hours' that do not exactly match the Total Operator Hours value shown/stored from the data-entry form (should be single source of truth; no report-side recomputation).
